response_constraints: Base the final answer only on returned tool results; do not imply that a skipped or failed call succeeded.
Respect the stage boundaries: calls grouped as parallel must be issued together in one assistant action, and later calls must wait for the IDs, status, date, or mutation result they consume.
Do not change any record outside the named scope; in particular, leave all fixture distractors untouched.
Do not use extra write or notification tools beyond the single action path explicitly requested.

stages.purpose: Resolve the two independent names in one parallel search action.
Retrieve the unique stage-1 matches together before checking the mutation condition.
Update New-student welcome message only after stage 2 verifies its unique ID and draft status.

arguments.body: New-student welcome message was updated to scheduled after status verification.
arguments.subject: Record update: New-student welcome message

stages.purpose: Notify the named operational recipient only after stage 3 returns a successful mutation.

state_assertions: Record mes-684 changes from draft to scheduled.
Record mes-284 and distractor mes-1084 remain unchanged.
Exactly one notification is sent to communications lead, and only after the mutation succeeds.

prompt: Resolve Year-end donor thanks in Volunteers and New-student welcome message in North Team with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If New-student welcome message is still draft, change only New-student welcome message from draft to scheduled. Leave Year-end donor thanks unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify communications lead with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. Do not touch similarly named, related, or archived records. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.

stage_4.body: New-student welcome message was updated to scheduled after status verification.
